title: Apparatus for building a carcass structure of a tyre for vehicle wheels
abstract: A process for manufacturing tyres for vehicle wheels, comprising the step of building on a forming support a carcass structure comprising at least one carcass ply and, at at least one end edge of the carcass ply, at least one annular anchoring structure comprising at least one substantially circumferential annular insert and at least one filling insert associated to said at least one substantially circumferential annular insert. The step of building the carcass structure comprises the steps of positioning the annular anchoring structure on the carcass ply by moving the annular substantially circumferential insert into contact with an end edge of the carcass ply by means of a special positioning member, pulling down the filling insert on the end edge of the carcass ply and turning up a free end portion of the end edge of the carcass ply so as to form a turned up end portion of the carcass ply including said annular anchoring structure. The pulling down of the filling insert on the end edge of the carcass ply is carried out by a special pulling down member while the substantially circumferential annular insert is retained into a contact position with the end edge of the carcass ply by said positioning member.
claims: 1. An apparatus for building a carcass structure of a tyre for a vehicle wheel on a forming support, the carcass structure comprising a carcass ply, and, at an end edge of the carcass ply, an annular anchoring structure comprising a substantially circumferential annular insert and a filling insert associated with the substantially circumferential annular insert, comprising: a positioning member of the annular anchoring structure on the end edge of the carcass ply, wherein the positioning member is configured to bring the annular anchoring structure in contact with the end edge of the carcass ply; a ring having a side surface with a predetermined profile, wherein the predetermined profile of the side surface of the ring is conjugated to a profile of a side surface of the filling insert when pulled down on the end edge of the carcass ply to act on the entire side surface of the filling insert annularly and radially; and a turning-up member of a turned up end portion of the carcass ply; wherein the ring is configured to slide with respect to the positioning member and is adapted to act on the filling insert while the positioning member retains the substantially circumferential insert in a contact position with the end edge of the carcass ply and before the turning-up member formed the turned up end portion of the carcass ply; and wherein the ring is controlled by a hydraulic cylinder with a piston, the piston passes through the positioning member to contact the ring, and wherein the side surface of the ring only moves along a direction parallel to a longitudinal axis of the piston.
